specs.surfaceSummary: User is replying to an open email thread in Gmail web (mail.google.com in Chrome) via the inline reading-pane reply composer.
specs.multiAuthor: Mostly single-author per email, but Gmail emits SENDER attribution that must be preserved (it is NOT chat-style per-line speakers). In the UIA --tree, the reading-pane email header exposes the sender as a `text`/`heading` node whose value is the display name plus a `Verified Sender`/email token (e.g. "Kiwi.com", "travel@kiwi.com"), and the recipient as "to me"; a multi-message thread renders each collapsed/expanded message as a sibling group under the conversation article, each with its own sender header. Attribution rule: take the sender display-name from the email-header block of each message group as the turn author; map "to me"/"You" mail you sent as author "You". Do NOT try to parse "Name:" speaker-prefix lines (that convention is Discord/Slack/Messenger only — Gmail bodies have no such prefix). For the common single-open-email case emit one conversation entry: {author: <sender display name>, text: <email body>}, plus threadSubject and replyTarget=that sender. There is no reliable per-author label inside the body itself, so attribution is per-message-group header, not per-line.
noiseToStrip: LEFT NAV (the headline leak): Compose, Labels, Inbox, Starred, Snoozed, Important, Sent, Drafts, Purchases, Social, Updates, Forums, Promotions and their unread counters (e.g. 'Inbox 2,677', 'Promotions 25,370', 'Updates 13,440'), 'Create new label', 'More', 'Personal'

INBOX MESSAGE LIST (middle column scrollback): every `tr.zA` row = sender + duplicated subject snippet + preview + date (e.g. 'Amazon.sa / Delivered: 1 item Order # 405-… / May 13', 'GoDaddy / Account Alert: …'). The whole inbox list must be dropped — only the OPEN email in the reading pane survives
Inbox-list metadata rows: 'Important', '1–50 of 7,109', '1-50 of …', 'Conversations', 'Everything else', date-only rows ('May 13', 'Jun 9', '10:42 AM')

OTP / verification-code emails that live in the inbox list (CRITICAL privacy leak): 'Google Verification Code … 622297', 'One time password … 7596', 'OTP CONFIRMATION', amazon/NVIDIA 'Authenticate Your Email Address', any 6-digit code rows. These come from OTHER inbox rows, not the open email, and must never reach the LLM
Gmail app chrome top bar: 'Skip to content', 'Using Gmail with screen readers', 'Gmail', 'Search', 'Search mail', 'Input tools on/off (Ctrl-Shift-K)', 'Select input tool', 'None selected'

Reading-pane action chrome: 'Show details'/'Hide details', 'Unsubscribe', 'Verified Sender', 'Pop out reply', 'Open email in browser', 'Subscription center', the '×'/'Inbox' label row, 'to me' (keep only as recipient hint, strip from body)
Composer toolbar/footer: 'Send', 'Describe your message', the formatting/attach/emoji button row

Object-replacement glyphs U+FFFC ('￼'), bullets, zero-width/joiner noise (the ' ͏ ͏ ͏'/' ‌ ‌ ‌' invisible-separator runs Gmail injects into preview snippets), and the duplicated subject-snippet ('Subject…Subject…' echo each row prints twice)
Browser/Chrome chrome: address+search bar, tab strip, bookmarks (already covered by GLOBAL omnibox/chrome rules)
pruneRules: GLOBAL (already present, keep): denoise_for_llm strips U+FFFC and other control/symbol glyphs and collapses inline whitespace; extend its noise class to also collapse Gmail's invisible-separator runs (U+034F COMBINING GRAPHEME JOINER, U+200C ZWNJ, U+0DF4-style filler) that pad preview snippets.

GLOBAL: focused-field-first landmark resolution (json_resolve_landmark) — find the focused composer node, climb its ancestor path to the nearest CONTENT landmark (doc/pane/group/article via json_landmark_role) and scope extracted content to that subtree, NOT the linear textBefore. This is the core fix; the current path falls through to beforeCaret because the chosen landmark is the whole doc.

GLOBAL: json_is_nav_chrome must drop `list` landmarks whose name matches JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE unless they contain the focus. KEEP this, and ensure the Gmail left-nav `list` (name like 'Navigation'/'Labels'/'Mailbox'/'Folders') and the inbox `list` (rows of senders) are both classified nav and dropped when the focus (composer) is NOT inside them.

GLOBAL: drop-by-role subtree pruning (json_drop_subtree_role) for toolbar/tabs/menu/menuitem/status/button/link/banner/image/tree/table — keep; this removes Gmail's compose toolbar, label buttons, and search box.
GLOBAL: json_is_omnibox drop — keep; also add Gmail 'Search mail' edit (already listed) so the search box never counts as content.

PER-APP (Gmail) override — content landmark identity: the reading-pane email lives in a group/article whose ancestor doc is the conversation view; prefer the landmark on the focus path that is a sibling of the composer (the open message body + its sender header), NOT the largest landmark (largest = whole doc incl. inbox list). When the focus-path landmark is the doc root, fall back to the deepest landmark on the path that excludes any `list` classified as nav/inbox.

PER-APP (Gmail) override — inbox-list detection beyond date-rows: replace/augment strip_list_scrollback. A `list`/`table` whose direct children are uniform 3-part rows (sender-name, subject-snippet that repeats, trailing short date 'May 13'/'10:42 AM') is the INBOX LIST → drop entirely. Do not rely on the 85%-position date-row cut (it leaves the 2-3 inbox rows that sit between the cut point and the open email, which is exactly the current Amazon.sa/GoDaddy leak).

PER-APP (Gmail) override — sender attribution: from the surviving reading-pane group, lift the sender display-name from the email-header block (the `heading`/`text` node adjacent to the 'Verified Sender'/email-address token and the '(N hours ago)' timestamp) as conversation[].author and as replyTarget; lift the subject (reading-pane heading, e.g. 'Thinking of adding travel insurance…') as threadSubject. Strip 'Verified Sender', 'Unsubscribe', 'Show details', the email-address token, and the timestamp from the body.

PER-APP (Gmail) override — strip the json_is_gmail_chrome_line set (already present) and extend it: 'verified sender', 'unsubscribe', 'open email in browser', 'subscription center', '1–50 of', 'conversations', 'important', 'select input tool', 'input tools on/off'.

GLOBAL: after pruning, the user's typed draft (composer contents) goes to `draft` (afterCaret/fieldText today is empty because the composer is blank at capture); when non-empty, the composer subtree text becomes `draft`, separate from the email body which becomes `replyTarget`/conversation.

steps: Open https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#inbox in its own new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs don't expose UIA content).

Settle ~2.5s for the Gmail SPA to boot, then POLL for the first inbox row: waitFor('tr.zA span.bog', 12000). Click it to open the email in the reading pane (do NOT double-click / open full-screen — inline reading pane is what exposes the inline composer).

POLL for the reading-pane Reply button: waitFor('span.ams.bkH', 12000) — this span ('Reply') only appears ~3-4s AFTER the email finishes rendering, so poll, never fixed-sleep. Click it to open the inline reply composer at the bottom of the reading pane.
POLL for the composer body: waitFor('[aria-label="Message Body"][role="textbox"]', 9000), then focus it and collapse the caret to end (focusEl: el.focus() + Range.selectNodeContents+collapse(false)).

Re-seat focus right before the UIA read (ensureExpr): the recipe focus is often lost to a Gmail re-render. Retry up to 4×: if document.activeElement matches the composeSelector, done; else querySelector the composer, click()+focus()+caret-to-end, wait 400ms. Confirm activeElement matches and its aria-label is 'Message Body'.

Page.bringToFront again, resolve the Chrome window HWND by title (title like 'Inbox (2,677) - …@gmail.com - Gmail - Google Chrome', sanitized: strip leading unread count, cut at first |<>"` char), then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <HWND> and --selection --hwnd <HWND>.
recipe.timingNotes: POLL, do not fixed-sleep: (1) inbox row tr.zA span.bog — poll up to 12s after a ~2.5s SPA-boot settle; (2) reading-pane Reply button span.ams.bkH — poll up to 12s, it appears ~3-4s after the email opens (this is the load-bearing delay); (3) composer [aria-label='Message Body'][role='textbox'] — poll up to 9s; (4) re-seat focus 4× at 400ms each before the read because Gmail re-renders steal focus. The UIA --tree read's inner watchdog is 750ms and the outer READ_TIMEOUT_MS is 1200ms; Gmail's full tree is large (~20KB axHtml, ~19KB textBefore) so the consumer must allow MAX_BUFFER_BYTES=4MB or the snapshot silently drops to empty. Element name 'Message Body' is the composer focus signal; window title carries the unread count + account email (sanitize before HWND lookup).

targetJson.description: Clean reply-ready context for an open Gmail email. The LLM receives the app/url/window metadata, the email being replied to (subject + sender + body), the reply target (sender), and the user's current draft — with the entire left-nav, inbox list, OTP rows, and Gmail/Chrome chrome removed. participants[] lists the sender + 'You'; conversation[] carries one entry per email in the open thread with per-message sender attribution. For the common single-open-email reply this is one conversation entry. Keys map onto context.rs JSON sections: app/url/window/field metadata + a structured reply block (threadSubject/participants/conversation/replyTarget/draft) replacing the leaky beforeCaret blob.

specs.notes:
VERIFIED against the real capture at E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/artifacts/context-cdp/gmail/rawSnapshot.json (textBefore=18,997 chars, axHtml=20,272 chars, elementName='Message Body', appExe='chrome.exe', url=''). The smoke gate (artifacts/context-cdp/gmail/smoke.json) FALSELY reports replyContextReady:true with beforeCaret=2,190 chars because it only checks depth+composer+no-OTP, not content provenance.

Reproduced the exact leak: after strip_list_scrollback the surviving beforeCaret (2,359 chars, 33 lines) is [tail of inbox list: Amazon.sa 'Delivered'/'Shipped', GoDaddy 'Account Alert'] + [the actually-open Kiwi.com email]. Two defects, both faithful to the artifact:
1) strip_list_scrollback (context.rs:703) cuts at the LAST inbox date-row in the first 85% of lines, leaving the 2-3 inbox rows between that cut and the open email. The Gmail inline composer sits at the BOTTOM of the reading pane, so linear textBefore necessarily prepends left-nav + inbox list — a linear cut can never be clean. The fix is the ax-prune landmark approach already half-built in context.rs (json_resolve_landmark / json_is_nav_chrome): scope to the reading-pane group on the focus path, drop the nav+inbox `list` subtrees, rather than slicing textBefore.
2) url is EMPTY in the capture ('url':'') — the Gmail canvas/nav heuristics that key off url-host won't fire; gate Gmail handling on window-title containing 'Gmail' + appExe chrome.exe/msedge.exe, not on url.

PRIVACY: the dropped inbox rows include live OTPs ('Google Verification Code … 622297', Qiwa OTP '7596'); the current linear path only avoids them by luck (they fall above the 85% cut). The landmark-scoped fix removes them structurally — important, because the smoke gate's otp_noise check runs on the OUTPUT and would (correctly) reject the payload if any OTP row survived, but it shouldn't rely on that backstop.

REFERENCE FILES NOTE: the spec task pointed at examples/winstt-electron/frontend/electron/lib/{ax-prune.ts,context-snapshot.ts} and scripts/context-harness/apps.ts — these do NOT exist in the repo (the examples/ tree is absent; per project memory the electron examples/handy clone was deleted, ref clone at E:/DL/Projects/.handy-ref). The harness tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs still hard-codes paths under examples/winstt-electron/.../winstt-context.exe and resolve-hwnd.ps1 (CONTEXT_EXE/RESOLVE_HWND consts, lines 36-55) — those binaries are presumably still present for the harness to have produced this capture, but the .ts sources to mirror are gone; the Rust port in context.rs IS the current source of truth and already mirrors the ax-prune approach (json_* functions). All recipe details above are taken faithfully from the embedded APPS.gmail recipe + COMPOSE map in context-cdp-capture.mjs.

specs.surfaceSummary: User is replying to a specific tweet on x.com: a single /status/<id> page is open, the original tweet (author + text) sits above an empty "Post your reply" composer with the caret seated, and a reply thread renders below.

steps: Navigate the dedicated window to https://x.com/home and Page.bringToFront (X needs the page 'visible' so Chromium builds the web a11y tree the UIA read depends on).
POLL for a real timeline tweet: waitFor('article[data-testid="tweet"]', 20000). Do NOT use a fixed sleep — the home timeline hydrates after SPA boot.

Click the first article[data-testid="tweet"]. This navigates to https://x.com/<author>/status/<id> (a single-conversation page), which carries the original tweet + reply box. Confirm by POLLing document.title changing to '<author> on X: "..." / X'.

POLL for the reply composer to mount: waitFor('[data-testid="tweetTextarea_0"]', 15000). On the status page the inline reply box ('Post your reply') is present without opening a modal — it is the SAME tweetTextarea_0 testid as the home composer.
Click [data-testid="tweetTextarea_0"] to reveal/seat, then settle ~600ms (Draft/Lexical editors do not focus on click alone).

Re-seat + verify focus right before the UIA read via ensureExpr('[data-testid="tweetTextarea_0"]'): click + el.focus() + collapse a Range to end, retry up to 4x, assert document.activeElement.matches the selector. This is the focus-miss guard — navigation focus is often lost to a re-render by capture time.

Resolve the Chrome window HWND by document.title slice (occlusion-proof) and run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <HWND> AND --selection --hwnd <HWND>. The composer is empty at dictation time so --selection is normally blank; the signal is in --tree's textBefore/axHtml.
recipe.timingNotes: Everything is POLL-based (waitFor), no fixed sleeps for readiness — only short settle delays (~600ms) after a click for the editor to commit focus. Two distinct waits: (a) the timeline tweet (article[data-testid=tweet], up to 20s for home hydration), (b) the reply composer on the status page (tweetTextarea_0, up to 15s). The UIA elementName for this composer renders as 'Post text' (9 chars) — verify field looks like a composer via that, not the raw testid. Window title is RTL/emoji-bearing ('<author> on X: "..." / X - Google Chrome'); sanitize before HWND-by-title (cut at quote/pipe, slice to ~30 chars). CRITICAL caveat: X's reply-box UIA TextPattern range spans the WHOLE article doc, so textBefore comes back rich (~470+ chars) and the pipeline takes the caret-split branch — it does NOT run the role-based ax-prune, so nav-rail + counts leak (see pruneRules).

targetJson.description: Clean structured reply context for an X/Twitter reply. The load-bearing object is replyTarget (the original tweet being replied to: author display-name + @handle + body text). participants[] lists the distinct authors of the rendered conversation (original author + reply-thread authors) with self flagged. conversation[] carries the visible reply thread in document order as {author,text} so the model knows who said what. threadSubject is omitted (X has no subject). draft is the user's current composer text (empty at dictation start). Self (the logged-in account) must NOT appear as a conversation turn. Engagement counts, the left nav rail, promoted/Ad tweets, 'Show translation'/'View quotes'/'Embedded video'/'Relevant' chrome, and the keyboard-shortcut banner are all stripped.

noiseToStrip: Keyboard-shortcut banner: 'To view keyboard shortcuts, press question mark' / 'View keyboard shortcuts'
Left nav rail items: Grok, Chat, Home, Explore, Notifications, Bookmarks, Creator Studio, Premium, 'Lift off', Profile, More, Post (the persistent sidebar, not content)
The logged-in user's own identity block in the top bar (e.g. 'Mostafa' + '@Dahshury') and the standalone 'Post'/'Conversation' section dividers
Engagement counts as bare-number / abbreviated lines: '232.9K', 'Views', '41', '82', '1.1K', '108', and inline reply-thread counts like '627', '2.2K', '5.4K'
Promoted content: any tweet whose block contains a standalone 'Ad' marker (e.g. 'Bitget TradFi ... Ad ...') — drop the whole promoted block
Per-tweet UI chrome: 'Show translation', 'View quotes', 'Embedded video', 'From <quoted-handle>', 'Relevant', 'Subscribe', 'Choose Files: No file chosen', the trailing action-row 'Reply' label
Composer placeholder echoed as content: 'Post your reply' (the empty-composer placeholder, not a turn)
Relative timestamps as standalone lines: '24m', '34m', '6m', '1h', '2h', 'Jun 14', '9:52 AM · Jun 14, 2026'

Browser/OS chrome from the axHtml tree: window/pane title bars, 'Minimize'/'Maximize'/'Close'/'Back'/'Forward'/'Reload', 'Address and search bar', 'Install X', 'Zoom: 80%', the entire Bookmarks toolbar (Work/Trading/Learn/Social/...)
Keep the 'Replying to @<handle>' marker ONLY as the signal that resolves replyTarget.handle — do not emit it verbatim as a conversation turn.
pruneRules: GLOBAL: focused-field-first — BUT this surface exposes the bug: X's reply composer TextPattern range spans the whole article, so textBefore is 'rich' (≥RICH_FIELD_MIN_CHARS=40) and format_context_for_prompt_json takes the caret-split branch (beforeCaret) WITHOUT running prune_ax_html_for_llm. PER-APP override needed: for x.com/twitter.com, when the focused field's caret text contains nav-rail markers (Home/Explore/Notifications co-occurring) OR a 'Replying to @' marker, route through prune_ax_html_for_llm on the axHtml instead of emitting the flat caret blob — the role-based pruner is what drops nav/toolbar/counts. Highest-impact fix for this surface.

GLOBAL: anchor on focus='1' (the 'Post text' edit), climb to the nearest CONTENT LANDMARK (doc/pane/group/article) holding the most scoped content (json_find_landmark_on_path). For X the landmark is the <doc> holding the tweet + thread.

GLOBAL: drop chrome SUBTREES by role — toolbar/tabs/tab/menu/menuitem/status/button/link/combo/check/radio/image/banner (json_drop_subtree_role). Removes the Bookmarks toolbar, window controls, the 'Address and search bar' edit (json_is_omnibox), and the nav-rail buttons in one pass.

GLOBAL: nav-list-vs-content-list disambiguation (json_is_nav_chrome) — a list/group named like 'navigation'/'timeline tabs'/'who to follow'/"what's happening" is dropped unless it contains focus or its name matches CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE (messages/conversation/timeline/thread/comments). For X keep the conversation list ('Timeline: Conversation') via the content-list hint; drop the left primary nav.

PER-APP (x.com): treat bare-number and abbreviated-count lines (^\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?[KMB]?$, and 'NNN Views') as engagement noise — extend JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE's count alternation (it covers '<n> likes/comments/shares/...' but X emits the count and the word 'Views' on SEPARATE lines, so add standalone bare-number + standalone 'Views'/'View quotes'/'Show translation'/'Embedded video'/'Relevant'/'Subscribe' to the low-signal drop list).

PER-APP (x.com): drop the keyboard-shortcut banner lines ('To view keyboard shortcuts...', 'View keyboard shortcuts') and the section dividers 'Post'/'Conversation' — add an X-specific chrome-line set (analogous to Gmail's CHROME_LINES) gated on x.com/twitter.com so other apps are untouched.
PER-APP (x.com): drop promoted tweets — when a reconstructed turn's block contains a standalone 'Ad' marker, suppress the whole block (mirrors the existing 'sponsored' low-signal rule; X uses 'Ad').

GLOBAL: relative-time + datetime lines are meta, not turns — JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE already drops '<n>[smhdw]' and 'H:MM AM/PM'; ensure 'Jun 14'-style bare month-day and 'H:MM AM · Mon DD, YYYY' are stripped from turn bodies (json_normalize_author already trims trailing times from author labels).

GLOBAL: self-suppression — the logged-in account block (display-name + @handle above the 'Post'/'Conversation' boundary, e.g. 'Mostafa'/'@Dahshury') is the user, not a turn; it sits in the nav/top-bar region and is dropped by the role-based prune when the landmark is the conversation <doc> rather than the whole page. Emit it only as the self field, never as a conversation entry.

GLOBAL: speaker-turn reconstruction (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns) emits 'displayName: body' per item/group/row — each tweet article becomes one turn; the FIRST kept turn (the original tweet, preceding 'Replying to @handle') is the replyTarget. The 'Replying to @<handle>' line is consumed as the replyTarget.handle signal, not emitted.

GLOBAL: cap + clip direction — with ≥3 speaker-like lines (json_should_clip_thread_tail) keep the TAIL (nearest the caret = most-recent thread); otherwise keep the HEAD so the original tweet (which appears FIRST) is never dropped. For x-reply the original tweet leads, so HEAD-clipping must preserve it — verify the replyTarget survives the JSON_MAX_LLM_CONTEXT_CHARS clip.
specs.multiAuthor:
X exposes authors NOT as 'Author:' prefixes but as two adjacent lines per tweet: a display name then an @handle (e.g. 'ساخر' then '@SakerSport'), with a 'Replying to @SakerSport' marker later for the reply target. Attribution is positional, not colon-delimited. Convention for THIS surface: (1) the ORIGINAL tweet = the author block (displayName + @handle) immediately preceding the longest body line and preceding the 'Replying to @handle' marker; that author/body is replyTarget. (2) self (logged-in account) = the displayName/@handle pair appearing in the top-bar/nav region BEFORE the 'Post'/'Conversation' boundary (here 'Mostafa'/'@Dahshury') — the user, NOT a turn; drop it. (3) reply-thread tweets below each follow displayName \\n @handle \\n <relative-time '24m'/'1h'/'Jun 14'> \\n <body> \\n <counts>. The Rust extractor reconstructs each as 'displayName: body' (then JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE matches), attributing by the (name, @handle) pair heading each tweet block. The single most important author/text pair is replyTarget; the rest are prior context.</multiAuthor>
<parameter name="notes">GROUND TRUTH: a real live capture exists at artifacts/context-cdp/x-reply/{rawSnapshot.json,smoke.json} (Arabic football tweet by @SakerSport). The smoke gate currently PASSES (replyContextReady:true, contextPayloadUsable:true, multiSpeakerContext:true, no warnings) — but that is the lenient gate's verdict, NOT a clean payload. The actual beforeCaret the LLM receives (473 chars, reproduced verbatim from rawSnapshot.json) still leaks the full left nav rail, the user's own @Dahshury identity, engagement counts (232.9K Views, 41, 82, 1.1K, 108), and chrome (Show translation / Embedded video / View quotes / Relevant). ROOT CAUSE: X's composer TextPattern range spans the whole article, so textBefore is 'rich' and format_context_for_prompt_json (E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs:1334) emits beforeCaret via the caret-split branch and never calls prune_ax_html_for_llm (the role-based pruner that would drop nav/toolbar/counts). 'CAPTURES reply-ready' is accurate (the original tweet author 'ساخر/@SakerSport' + text + 'Replying to @SakerSport' ARE present and the composer is focused), but 'clean' needs the per-app override in pruneRules (route x.com rich-but-nav-polluted caret text through the axHtml role-prune). KEY FILES (absolute): E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (format_context_for_prompt_json @1334, prune_ax_html_for_llm @1184, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns @976, JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE @628, json_focused_field_is_rich @736); proven focus recipe: E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs (APPS['x-reply'] @120) mirrored by examples/winstt-electron/frontend/scripts/context-harness/apps.ts focusXReply; quality gate: E:/DL/Projects/WinSTT/src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs (fixture_prompt_reply_ready @506, looks_like_composer_field @854, leaked_ui_chrome_terms @562).

specs.surfaceSummary: User is composing a brand-new top-level post on the X (Twitter) home timeline — draft only, no thread being replied to.
specs.multiAuthor: Single-author for THIS surface (home compose has no thread): conversation[] is empty and there is no replyTarget. (Author attribution only applies to the sibling x-reply surface, where the focused composer's nearest content landmark is the <doc> "Conversation" region and turns are flat lines under it — author = the @handle line that precedes a tweet body, e.g. "ساخر / @SakerSport" then body, then the reply list where each <item>/row's leading "@handle 24m" line is the author and the following line(s) are the text. On compose there is nothing to attribute.)

steps: Open https://x.com/home in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs/UIA tab-select do NOT build it).

POLL (not fixed sleep) for the compose box: waitFor('[data-testid=\'tweetTextarea_0\']', 20000). On x.com/home this collapsed box renders as 'What's happening?' but the timeline behind it is still spinning (see screenshot) — so wait for the element, not a timer.
Click the box once to expand it ([data-testid='tweetTextarea_0'].click()), then sleep ~800ms for the expanded composer + 'Everyone can reply' affordance to mount.

Re-query and focus: focusEl(document.querySelector('[data-testid=\'tweetTextarea_0\']')) — set caret to end via a collapsed Range so activeElement is the composer (a Draft.js contenteditable, NOT a native textarea).
Re-seat focus right before the UIA read with the ensureExpr retry loop (click+focus+caret-to-end, up to 4 attempts) — the X SPA re-renders the composer and silently drops focus.

CRITICAL FIX for this surface: resolve the window HWND from a STABLE title. On compose the document.title is just 'Home / X' (or 'X' while the timeline spins), so the harness fell back to titleHint ' / X', resolve-hwnd.ps1 returned NO_MATCH, and the native winstt-context.exe --tree read had NO window → empty rawSnapshot → empty prompt (replyContextReady=false). Resolve the HWND by the Chrome process's foreground window after bringToFront (or accept the 'Home / X - Google Chrome' OS title substring) rather than the SPA document.title; confirm a decimal HWND before invoking the sidecar.

Read context with the SAME native helper dictation uses: winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> (and --selection --hwnd <hwnd>). For compose, --selection is empty and the focused field (Draft.js div) yields little/no caret text — that is EXPECTED; the draft lives in the focused element, the (off) thread context does not exist.
recipe.timingNotes: POLL targets, never fixed sleeps: (1) compose box [data-testid='tweetTextarea_0'] — waitFor up to 20s (home timeline SPA boot is slow); (2) after the expand-click, the only reliable signal is the box becoming focusable, so ensureExpr's activeElement-matches loop (4×400ms) is the wait, not a timer. There is NO Reply button to poll on compose (that span.ams.bkH / 'Reply'-list-tail pattern belongs to the x-reply + gmail surfaces). The single hard requirement before the native read is a resolved decimal HWND — gate on that, with bringToFront re-issued after focus so the OS window title is 'Home / X - Google Chrome' and stays foreground for the occlusion-proof resolve-hwnd.ps1 lookup.

targetJson.description: Clean draft-only context for an X home-timeline compose. Because composer is focused but no thread is open, the LLM should receive ONLY: app/url/window/field metadata proving the surface, the user's current draft text (focused field), and explicitly NO conversation/replyTarget/threadSubject. participants[]=[the signed-in user] and conversation[]=[] so the model knows this is an original post, not a reply. The format_context_for_prompt JSON keys for THIS surface are: app, url, window, field, and (when the draft has text) fieldText — with screen/screenOcr/beforeCaret/afterCaret/selection/clipboard OMITTED (timeline-context off). The participants/conversation/replyTarget/draft view below is the LLM-facing logical shape the prompt encodes.
targetJson.exampleJson: {"app":"chrome.exe","url":"https://x.com/home","window":"Home / X - Google Chrome","field":"Post text","surface":"compose","participants":[{"handle":"@Dahshury","name":"Mostafa","self":true}],"conversation":[],"replyTarget":null,"threadSubject":null,"draft":"so excited to finally ship the new dictation context feature"}

noiseToStrip: Left nav rail items: Home, Explore, Notifications, Chat, Grok, Bookmarks, Creator Studio, Premium, Lift off, Profile, More, Post
Timeline tab strip: 'For you', 'Following', 'Machine Learning', 'Post-Labor Economics', 'Cursor' and any pinned-list tabs
'What's happening?' placeholder text and the 'Everyone can reply' / 'Everyone' audience selector
Composer action bar icons (media/GIF/poll/emoji/schedule/location) and the disabled 'Post' button
Right column entirely: 'Subscribe to Premium', 'Live on X', 'Today's News' / 'What's happening' / 'Trending now' / 'Trending in <country>' lists, 'Who to follow', Search box
Ad units anywhere (any node whose row contains 'Ad' / 'Sponsored', e.g. 'Bitget TradFi Ad', 'فهد العجلان Ad')
Engagement counters and reaction rows: bare numeric/K/M lines, 'Views', '232.9K Views', reply/repost/like/bookmark counts, 'View quotes', 'Relevant', 'Show translation', 'Show more'

Chrome browser chrome: 'Address and search bar' omnibox (x.com/...), Minimize/Maximize/Close/Back/Forward/Reload, 'Install X', 'Zoom: 80%', the entire 'Bookmarks' toolbar (Work/Trading/Learn/Social/...), tab strip + 'Tab search' / 'New Tab'
Boilerplate footer: 'Terms of Service | Privacy Policy | Cookie Policy | Accessibility | Ads info | More © 2026 X Corp.'
A11y/keyboard helpers: 'To view keyboard shortcuts, press question mark', 'View keyboard shortcuts', 'Choose Files: No file chosen'
Object-replacement glyphs (U+FFFC ￼), bullets, and emoji-only nodes
Any OTP/verification-code/login-skeleton text (login_or_skeleton_noise / otp gate) — not expected here but must never leak
pruneRules: GLOBAL: focused-field-first — find the focus path (json_find_focus_path on focus="1"); the Draft.js composer is role='edit' name='Post text'. On compose its caret text is the draft and there is NO content landmark above it worth climbing to (the only sibling landmark is the timeline feed = navigation/ads). So when the focused field is the composer and no real thread landmark exists, emit fieldText (the draft) + metadata ONLY; do not fall back to dumping the timeline <doc> as 'screen'. (PER-APP nuance: for x-reply the same climb lands on the <doc>/'Conversation' landmark which IS content and should be kept.)

GLOBAL: drop-by-role subtree removal (json_drop_subtree_role) — toolbar, tabs, tab, menu, menuitem, status, button, link, combo, check, radio, image, tree, table, thead, banner. This already strips the X action bar, audience combo, Post button, tab strip, and Chrome toolbar buttons.
GLOBAL: omnibox guard (json_is_omnibox) — drop the role='edit' whose name is 'Address and search bar'/'search'/'urlbar' so the x.com/... URL in the omnibox never becomes 'fieldText'.

GLOBAL: nav-chrome disambiguation (json_is_nav_chrome) — a role='list' whose name matches the nav vocabulary (sidebar/navigation/primary/timeline tabs/who to follow/what's happening) is dropped UNLESS it contains the focus node; the home timeline feed list is therefore dropped on compose because focus is in the composer, not the feed. Keep JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE (messages/conversation/comment thread/timeline) as the allow-exception only when it actually wraps the focus.

PER-APP override (x): add 'For you', 'Following', 'Premium', 'Live on X', 'Trending', 'Who to follow', 'Grok' to the nav/low-signal vocabulary so the right column and tab strip are pruned even though their role is a generic landmark/list, not 'toolbar'.

PER-APP override (x): treat any row/group whose normalized line ends with the ad marker (' Ad' token, JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE already covers 'sponsored') as low-signal and drop it — the X feed interleaves '… Ad' promoted posts ('Bitget TradFi Ad', 'فهد العجلان Ad').

GLOBAL: low-signal UI line filter (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE) — already strips standalone reply/like/comment/share/follow/'see more' and the '<N> likes|reposts|views|reactions' counter lines; ensure bare 'Views', 'View quotes', 'Relevant', 'Show translation', 'Show more', 'Subscribe' are added to it for X.

GLOBAL: denoise_for_llm — strip object-replacement chars (U+FFFC), bullets, control/symbol glyphs, collapse inline whitespace, drop empty lines. Already applied; confirm it removes the ￼ runs seen in the x-reply textBefore.

GLOBAL: time/meta line drop (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE) — '24m', '1h', '9:52 AM · Jun 14, 2026', 'online/offline/typing…'. Keeps author attribution clean on x-reply and removes timestamp noise everywhere.

GLOBAL: empty-capture honesty — when the resolved HWND is missing or the tree read returns nothing, format_context_for_prompt returns '' (empty), which the smoke gate scores as prompt_empty rather than a false 'ready'. Fix is in the recipe (resolve HWND from the OS window title, not the SPA document.title), NOT in the pruner.
specs.notes: Root-cause from the captured artifacts: x-reply succeeded (replyContextReady=true, multiSpeakerContext=true, keys=[app,beforeCaret,field,window], window resolved to the rich tweet title) but the x COMPOSE capture came back fully empty (rawSnapshot.json 0 bytes, hwnd '(none)', promptKeys []). DOM focus WAS achieved (focusResult.focused=true, ensureResult.attempt=0), so the composeSelector and click-to-expand recipe are correct. The single failure was HWND resolution: on x.com/home document.title is the bare 'Home / X', the harness sliced titleHint ' / X', and resolve-hwnd.ps1 found no decimal HWND, so winstt-context.exe --tree had no --hwnd target and read nothing. The implementable fix is recipe-side (resolve the Chrome OS window 'Home / X - Google Chrome' via the foreground window after bringToFront, gate the native read on a decimal HWND), not extractor-side. For this surface the IDEAL clean payload is intentionally THIN: app/url/window/field + the draft (fieldText). It must NOT fall back to dumping the home timeline feed as 'screen' (that is the noise the right column/ads/trending live in) — the pruner's focused-field-first + nav-chrome rules already produce that thin shape once the HWND is non-empty. The min-context-chars/lines gate for label 'x' (250 chars / 4 lines / 1 speaker) is calibrated for the REPLY surface; a pure compose draft will legitimately score replyContextReady=false (no thread, no speakers) and that is correct — compose is not a reply. Key source files: src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (format_context_for_prompt_json, json_is_nav_chrome, json_drop_subtree_role, prune_ax_html_for_llm), src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs (quality gate), tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs (APPS.x recipe + COMPOSE.x selector + resolveHwnd). Captured evidence: artifacts/context-cdp/x/{smoke.json,screenshot.png,rawSnapshot.json(empty)} and artifacts/context-cdp/x-reply/{rawSnapshot.json,smoke.json}.

specs.surfaceSummary: User is in a Discord DM (or server channel) with the most-recent conversation open and the caret seated in the message composer, dictating "reply to this" — the LLM must receive the rendered thread with per-author attribution plus the user's draft.
specs.multiAuthor:
Discord is MULTI-AUTHOR — per-turn attribution is mandatory (server channels interleave many speakers; DMs are 2-party). Discord's web UIA tree exposes authorship in TWO shapes, both already handled by json_reconstruct_speaker_turns in context.rs and asserted by the discord_* smoke tests:

1) FLATTENED item name (most common in the captured tree): each message row is one node `<item name="Author: message text"/>` where the author and body are already joined with ": ". These are emitted verbatim because they already match JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE (`^(@?Name|You|Me): \S`). Example: `<item name="Maya: The Windows build still needs signing."/>` → kept as `Maya: The Windows build still needs signing.`

2) SPLIT author/timestamp/body children: a message group `<item name="alice"><text>alice</text><text>Today at 2:14 PM</text><text>can someone review…</text></item>`. Here the AUTHOR is the item's `name` attribute (the leading <text> child duplicates it; the `Today at H:MM PM` / `H:MM AM` child is a timestamp). json_reconstruct_speaker_turns takes node.name (or the first text value) as the author via json_normalize_author, drops the timestamp child via JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE, drops the duplicate author line, and prefixes every remaining body line as `author: body`. Consecutive messages from the SAME author in Discord render as separate sibling <item> nodes that repeat the author name, so attribution survives grouping.

Attribution rule for the clean JSON: every conversation[] turn carries an explicit `author`. The current user's own turns surface with author "You" (Discord labels the self avatar/name "You" in the a11y tree and the empty trailing composer line is `You:`). DM `participants[]` = [me, the other user]; channel participants = the distinct authors observed in the rendered window (not the full member roster, which is dropped as nav). Author labels are the Discord display name / nickname as rendered, NOT the @username unless that is what UIA exposes.

steps: NAVIGATE to https://discord.com/channels/@me (DM home). For a server-channel capture, the recipe in tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs ('discord-server') instead resolves the first guild from [data-list-item-id^='guildsnav___'], navigates to /channels/<guildId>, then clicks text channels (a[data-list-item-id^='channels___']) until one has rendered messages (li[id^='chat-messages-'] length > 0).

DISMISS THE OPEN-IN-APP INTERSTITIAL FIRST (root cause of the empty capture in artifacts/context-cdp/discord: the page showed 'Discord App Detected / Open App / Continue in Browser', the DM list never rendered, focus stayed on BODY, snapshot was empty). Before the DM-list wait, POLL up to ~6s for a 'Continue in Browser' / 'Continue in browser' control and click it: clickFirst by visible text — scan a[role='button'], button, and anchors whose textContent matches /continue in browser/i; also try the known class/testid if present. Only proceed once the guilds rail [data-list-item-id^='guildsnav___'] OR a DM anchor a[href^='/channels/@me/'] is in the DOM.

ENSURE LOGGED IN: if location.pathname starts with /login or a 'Log In' form is present, the run cannot proceed — surface a focusMiss and stop (the dedicated capture browser must be pre-authenticated; task #9).

OPEN THE MOST-RECENT DM: waitFor('a[href^="/channels/@me/"]', 12000) and click the first one (skip the 'Friends'/'@me' home link — pick an anchor whose href has a numeric channel id segment, i.e. /channels/@me/<digits>). This is the existing apps.ts 'discord' recipe.

WAIT FOR THE THREAD TO RENDER (poll, don't sleep): waitFor('li[id^="chat-messages-"]', 10000) so the message list <ol> is populated. Discord virtualizes the scroller, so the rendered window already holds the most-recent N messages — no scroll needed for 'reply to this'.

SEAT THE CARET IN THE COMPOSER (poll): box = waitFor('div[role="textbox"][aria-label^="Message"]', 10000) || waitFor('div[role="textbox"]', 4000); then focusEl(box) (click + focus + collapse a Range to end). The aria-label is 'Message @Username' for DMs and 'Message #channel' for server channels — both start with 'Message', so the ^= selector covers both.

RE-SEAT RIGHT BEFORE THE UIA READ via ensureExpr('div[role="textbox"][aria-label^="Message"]') (the harness's 4-attempt click+focus+caret-to-end loop) because Discord re-renders the composer on channel switch and steals focus. Verify document.activeElement.matches the selector (NOT BODY) before reading.

CAPTURE with the Tree mode (winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd>): the dictation path uses ContextMode::Tree → axHtml → prune_ax_html_for_llm, which is what reconstructs the per-author speaker turns. Resolve the HWND by window title ('Discord') first; the composer's caret-context text fields are thin, so the axHtml tree (not beforeCaret) is the load-bearing capture for Discord.
recipe.timingNotes: POLL, never fixed-sleep, for these DOM signals: (1) the open-in-app interstitial 'Continue in Browser' control — appears immediately on first load in a fresh browser, gate the whole recipe on dismissing it; (2) the guilds rail [data-list-item-id^='guildsnav___'] and/or DM anchors a[href^='/channels/@me/'] (SPA boot, ~2-4s after navigate); (3) message rows li[id^='chat-messages-'] (the rendered thread — REQUIRED before reading, else the snapshot is shallow); (4) the composer div[role='textbox'][aria-label^='Message']. Channel switches re-render the composer, so the ensureExpr re-seat after a switch needs its retry loop (≥4 attempts, 400ms apart). Unlike Gmail (whose reading-pane Reply button span.ams.bkH only appears ~3-4s after the email opens and must be polled), Discord's composer is present as soon as the channel renders — the gating wait is the MESSAGE LIST, not a reply affordance. Give the SPA boot itself ~2.5s settle after bringToFront before the first poll. The failed artifacts/context-cdp/discord run also shows a CDP 'Runtime.evaluate timed out' risk on discord-server when guild navigation hard-reloads the page mid-evaluate; split the guild navigation and the channel/compose focus into SEPARATE evaluate calls so a location.href reload can't abort the focus IIFE.

targetJson.description: Clean structured context for a Discord reply. The LLM must see: which surface (DM vs channel), the participants, the FULL rendered recent conversation with per-author attribution (who said what), the explicit reply target (the message being replied to, if Discord's reply-affordance is engaged), and the user's current draft in the composer. ZERO Discord/browser chrome. In the current Rust pipeline this maps to format_context_for_prompt's JSON object: the reconstructed thread lands in `screen` (one `Author: text` per line via prune_ax_html_for_llm), the draft in `fieldText`/`beforeCaret`, plus `app`/`url`/`window`/`field` metadata. The fields below are the IDEAL normalized shape the extractor should converge toward; `conversation[]` is the structured form of what `screen` already encodes line-by-line.
targetJson.exampleJson:
{
  "app": "discord.com",
  "url": "https://discord.com/channels/784…/901…",
  "window": "#release | My Server - Discord",
  "field": "Message #release",
  "surface": "channel",
  "threadSubject": "#release",
  "participants": ["علي", "Maya", "Chris", "You"],
  "conversation": [
    { "author": "علي", "text": "The Arabic sender should stay attributed." },
    { "author": "Maya", "text": "The Windows build still needs signing." },
    { "author": "Chris", "text": "I uploaded the cert bundle." },
    { "author": "You", "text": "I will kick off the release after tests." }
  ],
  "replyTarget": { "author": "Chris", "text": "I uploaded the cert bundle." },
  "draft": ""
}

noiseToStrip: Browser chrome from the UIA <window>/<pane> root: <button name="Minimize|Maximize|Close|Back|Forward|Reload|View site information">, the <edit name="Address and search bar"> omnibox (json_is_omnibox drops it), the entire <toolbar name="Bookmarks"> subtree and every bookmark button
Discord left guilds/servers rail: <list name="Servers"> and any <list>/<group> whose name matches JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE (servers/sidebar/nav rail/roster/left rail) — the icon strip of guild buttons

Channel sidebar nav: <tree name="Channels"> / <node name="# general"> channel-list entries, and the DM conversation list a[href^='/channels/@me/'] roster — these are navigation, not the open conversation (JSON_NAV_NAME_RE: channels/direct messages/conversations)
Member list: <list name="Members"> and presence rows like 'Online 42', 'Online', 'Offline', 'Idle', 'Do Not Disturb' (JSON_NAV_NAME_RE members/participants + JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE online/offline)

Per-message timestamps and date dividers: 'Today at 2:14 PM', 'Yesterday at 11:02 AM', bare 'H:MM AM/PM', relative ages like '5m'/'2h'/'3d', and 'NEW'/'New messages' separators (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE, JSON_AUTHOR_TRAILING_TIME_RE)

System/meta event lines: 'X joined the channel', 'left the channel', 'started a call', 'missed a call', 'pinned a message', 'X reacted with :emoji:', 'changed the channel name', 'added/removed X to/from the channel' (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE)

Reaction pills and engagement counts: emoji reaction chips with counts, 'Add Reaction', and toolbar verbs 'Reply', 'Edit', 'Forward', 'React', 'Pin', 'More' that appear as hover message-action buttons (role=button → json_drop_subtree_role)
Composer chrome: the '+' upload button, GIF/sticker/emoji pickers, 'Send' — anything role=button inside the composer group; keep ONLY the <edit name="Message …"> text
Typing indicators ('Someone is typing…', 'typing…'), unread badges/pills, '@mentions' jump bar, the search box <edit name='Search'>
Object-replacement/emoji/icon glyphs (￼, bullets, private-use icon font codepoints) stripped by JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE/denoise_for_llm
The 'Open in App' / 'Discord App Detected' / 'Continue in Browser' interstitial text if it leaks into the tree before dismissal
OTP/verification noise if a security DM is open (e.g. a 6-digit login code) — defense already in the smoke quality gate (otp_or_login_code_noise_detected), keep it out of the conversation text
pruneRules: GLOBAL (focused-field-first): find the focus path via the focus="1" attribute (json_find_focus_path). The composer <edit name="Message …" focus="1"> is the anchor; climb its ancestor path to the nearest CONTENT landmark (json_find_landmark_on_path over roles doc/pane/group/article) and scope extraction to that landmark, EXCLUDING the focused edit itself so the draft isn't echoed into the thread context.

GLOBAL (omnibox guard): if focus resolves to the browser omnibox (json_is_omnibox: role=edit, name in {address and search bar, search, search mail, urlbar}) do NOT use it as the landmark — fall back to the largest content landmark (json_find_largest_landmark). Covers the failed-capture case where focus stayed on BODY/omnibox.

GLOBAL (drop chrome by role): json_drop_subtree_role removes entire subtrees for roles toolbar/tabs/tab/menu/menuitem/status/button/link/combo/check/radio/image/tree/table/thead/banner. This deletes Discord's guild buttons, channel <tree>, message-action buttons, the send button, and the omnibox/bookmark toolbar in one pass.

GLOBAL (content-vs-nav list disambiguation): json_is_nav_chrome keeps a <list> only if it is content. A list is NAV (dropped) when its name matches JSON_NAV_NAME_RE (channels/conversations/direct messages/members/participants/sidebar/…) UNLESS its name matches JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE (messages/conversation with/message thread/comment thread). Discord's message list is <list name="Messages"> or <list name="Messages in <channel>"> → CONTENT (kept); <list name="Servers">/<list name="Members"> → NAV (dropped). Critically, a nav-named container is still kept if it CONTAINS the focused composer (json_contains_node check) so the surrounding message group isn't accidentally pruned.

GLOBAL (speaker-turn reconstruction): for each <item>/<group>/<row> under the content landmark, json_reconstruct_speaker_turns derives the author from the node's name attribute (or, for item/row, the first descendant text) via json_normalize_author, collects descendant <text> values (json_collect_descendant_text_values, which itself drops nested toolbar/button/omnibox subtrees), dedupes consecutive lines, drops timestamp/meta lines (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE) and low-signal UI lines (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE), and prefixes each remaining body line as 'author: text' unless it already has a speaker prefix. This is what turns BOTH Discord shapes (flattened `name="Author: text"` and split author/timestamp/body children) into clean `Author: text` lines.

GLOBAL (author normalization): json_normalize_author rejects candidates that are empty, >48 chars, already a speaker line, or match nav/container/content-list regexes; trims trailing ', ' clauses, '… commented' suffixes, and trailing 'H:MM AM/PM' times; requires at least one alphabetic char (so a bare timestamp or count can't become an author). Keeps Unicode names (Arabic 'علي') intact — asserted by the discord smoke test.

GLOBAL (meta/low-signal line filter): JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE drops 'Today/Yesterday at H:MM', bare times, relative ages (Ns/Nm/Nh/Nd/Nw), and online/offline/typing… ; JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE drops join/leave/call/pin/react/rename/add/remove system events and 'N likes/reactions/…' counts and bare action verbs (reply/like/comment/share/send/follow). Both run on every collected line.

GLOBAL (thread-tail clipping near the reply): when the focus is known and ≥3 speaker-prefixed lines were collected (json_should_clip_thread_tail), keep the TAIL (most recent messages adjacent to the composer) up to JSON_MAX_LLM_CONTEXT_CHARS=12000 via clip_tail; otherwise clip_head. This guarantees 'reply to this' sees the latest turns, not the top of the scrollback.

GLOBAL (noise denoise): denoise_for_llm strips control/symbol/private-use/emoji codepoints (JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE) and collapses inline whitespace per line, dropping empty lines. Applied after collection.

PER-APP override (Discord composer label as the reply anchor): the composer <edit> name is 'Message #<channel>' (server) or 'Message @<user>' (DM) — looks_like_composer_field matches the leading 'Message' so the smoke gate's focusedFieldLooksComposer passes. No Discord-specific code branch is needed in context.rs today; Discord rides the GLOBAL chat-thread path (same as Slack/Teams/Telegram/Messenger), which is the intended design. The only PER-APP tuning point is ensuring 'Servers'/'Members' stay in JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE/JSON_NAV_NAME_RE (they already are) and that 'Messages in <channel>' stays matched by JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE 'messages' (it does).

PER-APP override (capture mode): Discord must be captured with ContextMode::Tree (axHtml). Its composer caret-context (textBefore/textAfter) is thin/empty, so json_focused_field_is_rich is false and the formatter takes the fallback-tree branch (json_build_fallback_tree_section → prune_ax_html_for_llm) — the speaker-turn thread lands in the `screen` JSON key. The user's in-progress draft, if any, still surfaces via fieldText/beforeCaret. This differs from Gmail, where a long reply body makes the field rich and beforeCaret carries the context.

specs.surfaceSummary: User is in a Discord server #channel backlog (many distinct authors) with the message composer NOT yet focused; they will dictate "reply to this" and expect the LLM to receive the recent channel conversation with per-author attribution plus their own draft.
specs.multiAuthor: Discord server channels are MULTI-AUTHOR — per-turn attribution is mandatory (this is the whole point of the surface). In Discord's web UIA tree each message group is a node (role item/group/li, often id="chat-messages-...") whose author is exposed two ways depending on render: (1) inline form — the node's name IS already "Author: text" (e.g. <item name="Maya: The Windows build still needs signing."/>), counted directly by the speaker-prefix regex; (2) split form — a per-message group/item node holds child <text> nodes in order [author display name, timestamp "Today at 2:14 PM", message body...] (e.g. <item name="alice"><text>alice</text><text>Today at 2:14 PM</text><text>can someone review...</text></item>). The extractor's json_reconstruct_speaker_turns handles BOTH: author = json_normalize_author(node.name) else first child <text>; remaining <text> children become the body; timestamp/meta lines are stripped; each surviving body is emitted as "Author: body". CRITICAL multi-author nuances for servers vs DMs: (a) Discord collapses CONSECUTIVE messages from the same author into one group with NO repeated author header on the 2nd+ line — those bodies must inherit the group's author (the per-group reconstruction already does this), so a single group can yield multiple "Author: line" turns all sharing one name; (b) different authors with the same display name must keep order and each stay attributed (covered by same_display_name_chat_turns_keep_order_and_valid_json); (c) bot/webhook messages carry a "BOT"/"APP" badge text node — treat the name as the author and drop the badge token; (d) the user's own turns appear as "You:". Attribution convention in the cleaned JSON: conversation[] entries are objects {author, text} with author = the Discord display name (or "You" for the user); the back-compat flat "screen" string uses "Author: text\n" lines that the smoke gate's speaker_like_line_count regex (^\s*(?:@?[\p{L}\p{N} _.'-]{2,40}|You|Me):\s+\S) counts. min_speaker_lines("discord")=2, so at least 2 distinct attributed turns must survive for multiSpeakerContext=true.

steps: Open https://discord.com/channels/@me in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true), then Page.enable + Runtime.enable + Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs do not get one).

GOTCHA (proven by artifacts/context-cdp/discord-server/screenshot.png): Discord may render an 'Open App / Continue in Browser' interstitial that hijacks the page and makes Runtime.evaluate hang (the discord-server run in summary.json failed with 'CDP Runtime.evaluate timed out' and an empty rawSnapshot). BEFORE the focus recipe, poll up to 6s for a 'Continue in Browser' / 'Continue in your browser' link or button and click it; only proceed once the guild rail (selector below) exists. Do this with awaitPromise + a hard 8s evaluate timeout so a stuck interstitial fails fast instead of timing out the whole sweep.

Wait (POLL, not fixed sleep) for the guild navigation rail: waitFor('[data-list-item-id^="guildsnav___"]', 15000). This is the left server-icon column; its presence means the app shell booted and the user is logged in.

Pick a server: read the first guild id via [data-list-item-id^="guildsnav___"] -> match /guildsnav___(\d{17,})/ -> navigate location.href='https://discord.com/channels/'+guildId. Then POLL for the channel sidebar: waitFor('a[data-list-item-id^="channels___"]', 8000).

Open a TEXT channel that actually has a rendered backlog: iterate a[data-list-item-id^="channels___"], click each, and after each click POLL (don't fixed-sleep) until document.querySelectorAll('li[id^="chat-messages-"]').length > 0 OR an [class*="messageListItem"] / [data-list-id="chat-messages"] item appears; stop at the first channel with >=2 message items so multi-author depth is available. Skip voice channels (no message list).

POLL for the message scroller to be populated: waitFor('[data-list-id="chat-messages"]', 8000) (the virtualized list container) and ensure at least 2 li[id^="chat-messages-"] children exist; if fewer, scroll the scroller up a little and re-poll so older turns from other authors render (servers need >=2 distinct authors for the smoke gate).

POLL for the composer and seat the caret: waitFor('div[role="textbox"][aria-label^="Message"]', 15000) -> el.click(); el.focus(); place caret at end via a collapsed Range. The aria-label is 'Message #<channel>' (e.g. 'Message #general'), which looks_like_composer_field() matches on 'message'.

Re-seat focus immediately before the UIA read using ensureExpr('div[role="textbox"][aria-label^="Message"]') — a Discord re-render frequently steals focus back to the document after the recipe; retry up to 4x click+focus+caret-to-end and verify document.activeElement matches.

Page.bringToFront once more, resolve the Chrome window HWND by title (resolve-hwnd.ps1; title is like '#general | <Server> - Discord ... - Google Chrome'), then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> and --selection --hwnd <hwnd>. Feed --tree stdout to context_prompt_smoke --label discord --require-prompt-json.
recipe.timingNotes: Everything is POLL-based via waitFor (150ms steps), not fixed sleeps, because SPA boot + virtualized message render are non-deterministic. Known waits to POLL for, in order: (1) 'Continue in Browser' link OR guild rail [data-list-item-id^="guildsnav___"] (interstitial can add ~1-3s before the shell mounts); (2) channel sidebar a[data-list-item-id^="channels___"] after guild navigation (~1-3s); (3) populated scroller [data-list-id="chat-messages"] with >=2 li[id^="chat-messages-"] after clicking a channel (Discord virtualizes — the list can be empty for ~1-2s and only fills on scroll); (4) composer div[role="textbox"][aria-label^="Message"]. Only the SPA-boot settle (~2.5s after bringToFront) and a small post-focus settle are time-based; all content gates are conditions. Re-verify composer focus with ensureExpr right before the UIA read because Discord re-renders steal focus. Hard-cap each Runtime.evaluate at 8s so a wedged interstitial (the observed failure mode) fails fast.

targetJson.description: Clean structured context for a Discord SERVER #channel reply. The LLM must see the thing being acted on (the recent channel backlog with PER-AUTHOR attribution across many distinct authors), the reply target (last non-You message, or an explicitly-quoted/replied message when present), the channel/server identity, and the user's current draft — with zero Discord chrome (server rail, channel list, member roster, reactions, join/leave system rows, typing indicators). app/ide/url/window/field mirror the existing metadata keys; conversation[] is the per-turn attributed thread; the flat 'screen' string is the back-compat speaker-prefixed rendering the current extractor and smoke gate consume.
targetJson.exampleJson:
{"app":"chrome.exe","ide":false,"url":"https://discord.com/channels/884712/990321","window":"#release | Acme Devs - Discord","field":"Message #release","server":"Acme Devs","channel":"#release","participants":["Maya","Chris","علي","You"],"conversation":[{"author":"علي","text":"The Arabic sender should stay attributed."},{"author":"Maya","text":"The Windows build still needs signing."},{"author":"Maya","text":"I can push a fresh cert request this afternoon."},{"author":"Chris","text":"I uploaded the cert bundle to the shared drive."},{"author":"You","text":""}],"replyTarget":{"author":"Chris","text":"I uploaded the cert bundle to the shared drive."},"draft":"","screen":"علي: The Arabic sender should stay attributed.
Maya: The Windows build still needs signing.
Maya: I can push a fresh cert request this afternoon.
Chris: I uploaded the cert bundle to the shared drive.","selection":""}

noiseToStrip: Server (guild) icon rail — list/tree named 'Servers' or nodes from [data-list-item-id^="guildsnav___"]; pure navigation, drop entirely
Channel sidebar — list/tree named 'Channels' (a[data-list-item-id^="channels___"], '# general', '# random', category headers, unread pips); nav, drop
Member roster / online sidebar — list named 'Members'/'Participants' and rows like 'Online 42', 'Offline', role-group headers; drop

System rows — '<name> joined the channel', 'left the channel', 'pinned a message', 'started a call', 'missed a call', 'reacted with <emoji> to <name>', 'changed the channel name', 'added/removed <name> to/from the channel' (covered by JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE)
Reaction pills + counts — emoji + count chips under a message, 'Add Reaction' buttons; drop (role button + low-signal)
Per-message hover action toolbar — Reply / React / Edit / More / Forward buttons (role toolbar/button); drop
Timestamps + relative meta — 'Today at 2:14 PM', 'Yesterday at 9:03 AM', '11:02 AM', 'Edited', '3m', date-divider rows 'June 14, 2026' (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE / JSON_AUTHOR_TRAILING_TIME_RE)
Typing indicator — '<name> is typing...', 'Several people are typing...'; drop
Bot/app badges — 'BOT', 'APP' tokens appended to author names; strip the badge, keep the author
Composer chrome — 'Upload a File', emoji/GIF/sticker/gift buttons, 'Message #channel' placeholder echoed as content, slash-command popouts; keep only the user's typed draft
Browser chrome — Chrome tab strip titles, 'Address and search bar' omnibox value, other tabs' Gmail/Discord/Facebook titles (browser_tab_strip_titles_do_not_leak test); drop

Interstitial — 'Discord App Detected', 'Open App', 'Continue in Browser', 'Do you want to open this link in your Discord app?' (the artifact screenshot's modal); must never reach the LLM — it means capture happened before the channel loaded
Object-replacement / emoji-as-image glyphs and control chars (￼, ✦, etc.) — stripped by JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE in denoise_for_llm
OTP/login-code text and login/skeleton pages (defense-in-depth — covered by the smoke gate; should never appear in a real channel)
pruneRules: GLOBAL (already in context.rs): focused-field-first — find the focus path via focus="1" (json_find_focus_path); if the focused field is RICH (>=40 cleaned chars of textBefore/textAfter/focusedText) emit beforeCaret/afterCaret and DROP the ax tree. For Discord the composer is usually EMPTY at 'reply to this' time, so the field is thin and the pipeline falls through to the ax-tree (screen) path — this is the expected branch for this surface.

GLOBAL (json_resolve_landmark): climb the focus path to the nearest CONTENT landmark (role doc/pane/group/article via json_landmark_role) whose scoped cleaned content >= JSON_LANDMARK_MIN_CHARS (20); else pick the largest landmark in the tree. For Discord, the focus path runs composer -> 'Message composer' group -> 'Messages' group/pane, so the resolved landmark is the message scroller, NOT the whole app — this is what excludes the server rail / channel list / member roster.

GLOBAL (json_drop_subtree_role): drop entire subtrees by role: toolbar, tabs/tab, menu/menuitem, status, button, link, combo, check, radio, image, tree, table, thead, banner. PER-APP relevance: this kills Discord's channel 'tree', the hover action 'toolbar'/'button's, reaction 'button's, and the omnibox; the member roster and server rail are 'list' nodes handled by the nav-name rule below.

GLOBAL (json_is_nav_chrome + JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE): a role=list whose name matches nav vocab (channels, members, participants, servers, direct messages, sidebar, navigation, ...) is treated as nav and dropped UNLESS it contains the focused node. JSON_NAV_NAME_RE already includes 'channels?' and 'members?|participants?' and JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE includes 'servers?|roster|left rail' — so Discord's 'Channels', 'Members', and 'Servers' lists are pruned. The content-vs-nav disambiguation: JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE ('messages?|conversation with|message thread|...') force-KEEPS a list named 'Messages' / 'Messages in <channel>' even though it is a list — this is why the channel backlog survives.

GLOBAL (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns): for role item/group/row nodes, reconstruct per-author turns — author from node.name (json_normalize_author) else first child <text>; remaining child <text> values are the body; timestamp/meta and low-signal lines filtered; emit 'Author: body'. This is the multi-author engine and needs NO Discord-specific change — discord_split_author_nodes_reconstruct_speaker_turns and discord_thread_keeps_multi_sender_message_context already pass for both inline and split shapes.

GLOBAL (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE): drop join/leave/pin/call/react system rows and engagement-count lines, but the speaker-prefix guard (json_is_low_signal_ui_line checks NOT speaker-prefixed first) protects a real message that happens to contain a keyword.

PER-APP override RECOMMENDED (new, to lift this surface from the current failing state): the discord-server harness run produced an EMPTY rawSnapshot (interstitial wedge) so nothing was pruned — fix is in the RECIPE (dismiss interstitial, poll for >=2 rendered chat-messages items before the UIA read), not the pruner. Once real UIA arrives, the existing GLOBAL rules already yield the correct cleaned thread (validated by the discord unit tests).

PER-APP override OPTIONAL (enrichment, not required for the gate): when building the structured conversation[], derive 'server' from the window title segment after '|' (e.g. '#release | Acme Devs - Discord' -> server='Acme Devs', channel='#release'), and set replyTarget to the last non-'You' attributed turn (or, if a UIA 'Replying to <author>' quote node is present above the composer, use that quoted author+text). These map into new optional JSON keys (server/channel/conversation/replyTarget/participants/draft) and must be additive — keep emitting the existing app/url/window/field/screen keys unchanged so context_prompt_smoke's speaker_like_line_count and min_context_chars("discord")=250 / min_context_lines=4 checks still pass.

GLOBAL (omnibox guard json_is_omnibox): if focus landed on the Chrome 'Address and search bar' instead of the composer, ignore it as the landmark anchor and fall back to the largest content landmark — covers the focus-miss case where the recipe failed to seat the caret.
specs.notes: Reference Electron files (examples/winstt-electron/.../apps.ts, STATUS.md, ax-prune.ts, context-snapshot.ts, context-parsing-roadmap.md) are ABSENT from this checkout — the examples/winstt-electron tree does not exist (only the winstt-context.exe path is referenced by the harness). Spec was derived from the live Rust port at src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs (which is the 1:1 port of ax-prune.ts/context-snapshot.ts and carries the discord/slack/messenger unit tests that encode the exact UIA shapes), the quality gate at src-tauri/src/bin/context_prompt_smoke.rs, and the proven discord-server recipe + COMPOSE selector in tools/context-cdp-capture.mjs. KEY FINDING: the latest sweep (artifacts/context-cdp/summary.json) shows discord-server FAILED with 'focus error: CDP Runtime.evaluate timed out' and a 0-byte rawSnapshot.json; artifacts/context-cdp/discord-server/screenshot.png shows the cause — a 'Discord App Detected / Open App / Continue in Browser' interstitial wedged the page before any channel loaded. The pruner is already correct for Discord servers (multi-author tests pass); the surface's fix is RECIPE-side: dismiss the interstitial, then poll for >=2 rendered li[id^=chat-messages-] items before seating the composer caret and running the UIA read.

specs.surfaceSummary: User is replying inside an open Facebook Messenger conversation (facebook.com/messages/t/ or messenger.com/t/) with the caret seated in the message composer.
specs.multiAuthor: Multi-author. Messenger 1:1 and group DMs are per-author attributed. Two UIA author conventions appear (both already handled by json_reconstruct_speaker_turns in context.rs): (1) CANONICAL inline-body form — `<item name="Maya Chen">Hey, are we still on for Friday's standup?</item>` where the author display name is the item's `name` attribute and the message body is the item's inline text (verified by test messenger_item_name_with_inline_body_reconstructs_speaker_turns). (2) PACKED form — `<item name="Dana: Are we still meeting at 4 PM?"/>` where `Author: body` is collapsed into the `name` (verified by test facebook_messenger_keeps_chat_and_drops_nav). The current user's own turns are labeled `You`. Attribution rule: take author from item `name` (inline-body form) OR from the leading `Author:` prefix already present (packed form); when an item splits into child `<text>` nodes (author/timestamp/body), the FIRST text child is the author and the timestamp text node is dropped. Consecutive messages from the same author may arrive as separate sibling items with the SAME `name` — keep each as its own turn, preserve order. Group DMs surface 3+ distinct names; emit `Author: text` per turn so the LLM knows who said what.

steps: Open https://www.facebook.com/messages/t/ in its OWN new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs do not get one).

POLL for the SPA to leave the skeleton loader — do NOT fire the focus recipe on a fixed sleep. The last capture failed with 'Cannot find default execution context' + a grey skeleton screenshot because the recipe ran mid-navigation. Gate on: waitFor("div[role='grid'] [role='row'] a[href*='/messages/t/']", 25000) AND confirm the skeleton placeholders are gone (no element matching div[role='gridcell'] [style*='shimmer'] / the grey pulse bars; in practice poll until the conversation-list rows have real aria-labels with names, not empty).

Click the first REAL conversation row: clickFirst(['div[role=\'grid\'] [role=\'row\'] a[href*=\'/messages/t/\']','a[href*=\'/messages/t/\'][role=\'link\']']). This navigates the right pane to an actual thread.

POLL for the thread message log to render (this is the equivalent of Gmail's span.ams.bkH appearing ~3-4s late): waitFor("div[role='main'] div[aria-label^='Messages in conversation'], div[role='main'] [role='log'], div[aria-label*='conversation']", 15000). The message rows themselves are div[role='row'] under that log — wait until at least 2 are present so the thread has depth before the read.

POLL for the composer and seat the caret: const box = await waitFor("div[role='textbox'][contenteditable='true'][aria-label='Message'], div[aria-label='Message'][role='textbox'], div[role='textbox'][contenteditable='true']", 12000); focusEl(box). Messenger sometimes labels it via aria-placeholder='Aa' instead of aria-label='Message' — fall back to the bare div[role='textbox'][contenteditable='true'] inside div[role='main'] (scope to main so the SEARCH textbox in the left rail is never picked).

Re-seat focus with ensureExpr(composeSelector) right before the UIA read (the recipe focus is routinely lost to a re-render — mirrors every other app in context-cdp-capture.mjs), then Page.bringToFront once more.

HARDEN HWND resolution before the native read: the window title is 'Messenger | Facebook' (or '(N) Messenger | Facebook'). The current resolveHwnd sanitizer splits on '|' and keeps the clean leading slice 'Messenger', which is correct — but the last sweep still returned hwnd=(none) for every non-Gmail app, so verify resolve-hwnd.ps1 actually matched the dedicated browser's foreground window; if not, pass the explicit --hwnd from the CDP-known window rather than re-resolving by title. A 0-byte rawSnapshot.json means the --hwnd was wrong/empty — treat that as a hard failure, not a pass.
recipe.timingNotes: POLL, never fixed-sleep. Three distinct waits: (1) conversation-list ready — waitFor a[href*='/messages/t/'] with a real name, up to 25s, AND skeleton-gone (grey shimmer/pulse bars removed); the prior failure was firing focus while still on the skeleton. (2) After clicking a row, POLL for the thread log div[aria-label^='Messages in conversation'] / [role='log'] AND >=2 message rows (this is Messenger's late-render, analogous to Gmail's span.ams.bkH ~3-4s delay). (3) POLL for the composer div[role='textbox'][contenteditable='true'][aria-label='Message'] up to 12s. Only after all three resolve do bringToFront + ensure-focus + the native --tree/--selection read. If document.readyState is still 'loading' or Runtime.evaluate throws 'Cannot find default execution context', back off and retry the focus IIFE rather than proceeding to the read.

targetJson.description: Clean structured JSON the LLM receives for a Messenger reply. Produced by format_context_for_prompt_json in context.rs: metadata keys (app/url/window/field) plus a 'screen' string that prune_ax_html_for_llm reconstructs from the focused conversation landmark as newline-joined 'Author: message' speaker turns (one per line, chronological), with the left chat-list nav, message-action chrome, reactions, and timestamps stripped. The composer draft, if any, arrives via beforeCaret/fieldText (usually empty for a fresh 'reply to this'). conversation[] = ordered turns; participants[] = distinct authors seen; replyTarget = the last non-'You' turn (the message being replied to); threadSubject = the conversation/person name from the list landmark name ('Messages in conversation with <Name>'); draft = current composer text.

noiseToStrip: Left conversation-list rail: <list name="Chats"> / <list name="Conversations"> and its rows (e.g. 'Dad', 'Mom. Did you eat? 8:15 AM') — these are OTHER threads, not the open one; drop the whole subtree (json_is_nav_chrome on a nav-named list NOT containing focus).
Search box in the left rail (<edit name="Search Messenger"/> / 'Search') — never pick it as the composer; scope composer lookup to div[role='main'].
Per-message timestamps and presence: 'Today at 2:14 PM', 'Active 5m ago', 'Online', 'Sent', 'Seen', 'Delivered', '2h', '8:15 AM' (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE / JSON_AUTHOR_TRAILING_TIME_RE).
Reaction + message-action chrome: 'React', 'Reply', 'Forward', 'More', emoji reaction pills, 'Press Enter to send', 'Aa' placeholder (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE).

System/event rows: 'X started a call', 'Missed call', 'X named the group', 'X added Y to the group', 'You waved at X', 'X reacted ❤ to your message', 'X is typing…' (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE event clauses + 'typing...').

Facebook top nav / app chrome when on facebook.com/messages: Home/Watch/Marketplace/Groups/Gaming tabs, notifications, account-switcher (<toolbar>/<tabs>/<banner>/<nav> roles dropped by json_drop_subtree_role).
Browser chrome: 'Address and search bar' omnibox, tab-strip titles like 'Messenger | Facebook - Part of group social', other tabs leaking Gmail/X/Discord (json_is_omnibox + tabs/tab subtree drop).
Avatars / images / 'profile photo' image nodes (role=image dropped).
Object-replacement chars ￼ and decorative emoji/symbols (JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE).

Login/skeleton noise if not signed in or still loading: 'Log in', 'Create new account', shimmer placeholder rows (login_or_skeleton_noise gate in the smoke binary rejects these as unusable — the capture must wait them out, not emit them).
pruneRules: GLOBAL (focused-field-first): find the focus path via focus="1"; if the focused field itself is rich (caret text >= 40 chars) emit beforeCaret/afterCaret and SKIP the tree. For Messenger the composer is empty on a fresh reply, so the thin-field path runs and prune_ax_html_for_llm produces the 'screen' turns. (json_focused_field_is_rich)

GLOBAL (climb to nearest content landmark): from the focused composer, walk ancestors and pick the nearest landmark-role node (doc/pane/group/article) whose scoped content length >= 20 chars; else the largest landmark in the tree. For Messenger this resolves to <group name="Conversation with X"> or the <list name="Messages in conversation with X"> wrapping the focused edit. (json_resolve_landmark / json_find_landmark_on_path)

GLOBAL (drop chrome by role): drop entire subtrees for roles toolbar/tabs/tab/menu/menuitem/status/button/link/combo/check/radio/image/tree/table/thead/banner. This removes Messenger message-action buttons, reaction pills, avatars, the FB top-nav tab strip, and the browser tab strip. (json_drop_subtree_role)

GLOBAL (omnibox guard): never emit an <edit> whose name is 'address and search bar'/'search'/'search mail'/'urlbar'; fall back to page content. EXTEND PER-APP: also treat 'Search Messenger' / left-rail 'Search' as a non-content edit so it is never mistaken for the composer. (json_is_omnibox — add the Messenger search name)

GLOBAL (content-vs-nav list disambiguation): a list is NAV (dropped) when its name matches chats/conversations/inbox/channels/members/sidebar/navigation AND it does NOT contain the focused node; a list is CONTENT (kept) when its name matches messages/conversation with/message thread. This is exactly what keeps <list name="Messages in conversation with Maya Chen"> and drops <list name="Chats">. (json_is_nav_chrome + JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE + JSON_NAV_NAME_RE) — verified by test facebook_messenger_keeps_chat_and_drops_nav.

GLOBAL (speaker-turn reconstruction): for item/group/row nodes, derive the author from node.name (inline-body form) OR from a leading 'Author:' already in the text (packed form) OR, when the item has child <text> nodes, from the FIRST text child; drop the timestamp text node; emit each body as 'Author: body'. Same-author consecutive siblings each become their own turn in order. (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns) — verified by tests messenger_item_name_with_inline_body_reconstructs_speaker_turns and facebook_messenger_keeps_chat_and_drops_nav.

GLOBAL (line-level noise filter): drop lines matching time/meta (Today at H:MM, H:MM AM, Nd/Nh/Nm, online/offline/typing...) and low-signal UI/event lines (React/Reply/Share/Seen, engagement counts, 'started a call', 'added/removed … to the conversation', 'reacted with'). (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE, JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE) — verified by chat_system_noise_is_dropped_without_dropping_thread_words and facebook_engagement_counts_are_dropped_from_feed_context.

GLOBAL (thread-tail clip): when the focused field is present and >=3 speaker-prefixed lines are observed, keep the RECENT tail (clip_tail) up to JSON_MAX_LLM_CONTEXT_CHARS=12000 so the latest turns nearest the reply survive on long threads. (json_should_clip_thread_tail)

PER-APP OVERRIDE (Messenger has NO override needed today): the URL host facebook.com/messages/* and messenger.com/* already flow through the generic chat path; do NOT add it to is_canvas_surface (the conversation IS in the UIA tree, unlike Figma/Sheets). The only Messenger-specific tweak is extending the omnibox/search-edit guard with the 'Search Messenger' name so the left-rail search is never selected as the composer.

CAPTURE-SIDE (not a pruner rule, but the actual current gap): the extractor is already correct for Messenger per the unit tests — the failure is upstream. Require a non-empty, well-formed rawSnapshot (rawJsonValid) and a non-skeleton page before judging; a 0-byte snapshot or login_or_skeleton_noise must be retried via the polling recipe, never passed through to the LLM.

specs.surfaceSummary: User is composing a comment under a specific post in the Facebook home feed (the post's inline comment box, [aria-label="Leave a comment"]); "reply to this" must reply within that post's comment thread.
specs.multiAuthor: Multi-author. Facebook feed UIA (Chrome --tree) flattens each post into an `<article>` landmark whose author appears as the post header text and again in the `name` of the post `<article name="...">` or as the first `<text>`/`<item>` child; each existing comment is a separate `<item>`/`<group>`/`<row>` whose author name is either (a) inline in the node `name="Author: body"` form, or (b) a split sub-node — a leading `<text>`/`<link>` carrying the commenter's display name (a profile link) followed by sibling `<text>` nodes carrying the comment body and a relative timestamp ("3h", "1d"). Attribution rule, mirroring the existing json_reconstruct_speaker_turns: treat the `<article>` as the post turn (author = post-header name, normalized via json_normalize_author which already strips " commented", trailing times, and a trailing ",city" segment); treat each comment `<item>/<group>/<row>` as one turn whose author = node.name (or first descendant text that normalizes to a name) and body = the remaining descendant `<text>` values, emitting "Author: body".
