The current user's own draft comment is the focused `<edit>`/textbox named "Leave a comment"/"Write a comment" and is NOT a turn — it is the draft. There is no reliable explicit reply-target node for a top-level comment, so replyTarget defaults to the post itself unless the composer is nested inside a specific comment's reply box (aria-label "Reply to <Name>").
noiseToStrip: Left nav rail: Home / Friends / Marketplace / Groups / Video / Memories / Saved / Pages / Dashboard / See more
Right rail: Sponsored ads, Birthdays, Contacts/active-now list, 'People you may know'
Top bar: 'Search Facebook' omnibox, the blue tab strip (Home/Watch/Marketplace/Groups), Messenger/notifications/account buttons
Composer chrome on the post: 'What's on your mind', Live video / Photo/video / Feeling/activity create-post buttons

Engagement counts and reaction rows: 'NN comments', 'NN shares', 'NNK likes', 'All reactions', the Like/Comment/Share action button row, 'Most relevant'/'Top comments'/'View more comments'/'Write a comment...' placeholder
Per-comment affordances: 'Like', 'Reply', 'Share', 'See more', 'See translation', 'Edit', 'Hide', '· Edited', reaction emoji pickers
Relative timestamps on comments and posts ('3h','1d','Just now','Yesterday at 4:15 PM') as standalone lines
'Sponsored' / 'Suggested for you' promoted posts interleaved in the feed
Modal/interstitial chrome: 'Remember Password'/'Save info' Chrome prompt, cookie banner, 'Turn on notifications', login-wall ('Email or phone', 'Forgot password', 'Create new account')
Chrome browser chrome: window control buttons, Back/Forward/Reload, 'Address and search bar', Bookmarks toolbar, Tab strip / other tab titles (these can leak OTHER logged-in accounts)
OTP/verification-code text and 6-digit codes if a security post/notification is on screen
pruneRules: GLOBAL: focused-field-first — find the node with focus="1" (the 'Leave a comment'/'Write a comment' edit/textbox), record its path; this is the draft anchor and seeds landmark resolution (json_find_focus_path).

GLOBAL: climb the focus path to the nearest content landmark by role — landmark roles = doc|pane|group|article (json_landmark_role). PER-APP override: for facebook-feed prefer the enclosing `<article>` (the single post) over the page-level `<doc name="Facebook">`, so context is scoped to THE post being commented on, not the whole wall — json_find_landmark_on_path already picks the on-path landmark with the most scoped content, which lands on the article.

GLOBAL: drop whole subtrees by role — toolbar|tabs|tab|menu|menuitem|status|button|link|combo|check|radio|image|tree|table|thead|banner (json_drop_subtree_role). This removes the create-post buttons, reaction pickers, the Like/Comment/Share button row, the right-rail ad images, and the Chrome bookmarks toolbar/tab strip.
GLOBAL: drop the omnibox edit (name in {'address and search bar','search','search mail','urlbar'}) so the 'Search Facebook' box and URL never leak (json_is_omnibox).

GLOBAL: content-vs-nav list disambiguation — a `<list>` whose name matches nav terms (JSON_NAV_NAME_RE / JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE: sidebar, navigation, shortcuts, pages, contacts, who-to-follow, what's-happening) is dropped UNLESS it contains the focused node; a list whose name matches content terms (JSON_CONTENT_LIST_NAME_RE: comments, comment thread, timeline) is kept (json_is_nav_chrome). PER-APP add: ensure 'Comments'/'Comment by'/'comment thread' stays whitelisted and 'Sponsored','Suggested','Stories','People you may know','Birthdays','Contacts' get nav-dropped.

GLOBAL: speaker-turn reconstruction for item|group|row nodes — author from node.name or first descendant text via json_normalize_author, body from remaining descendant `<text>` values, emit 'Author: body' (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns). This is what turns the split comment nodes (profile-link name + body + '3h') into 'Omar: I can review the deck tonight.'

GLOBAL: low-signal UI line filter drops 'NN comments/likes/shares', 'Reply','Like','Comment','Share','Send','Follow','See more','Sponsored','Write a comment' (JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE) and standalone relative-time/meta lines '3h','1d','Today at 4:15pm' (JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE) — already covered, keep facebook engagement-count variants in the regex.

GLOBAL: privacy gates — drop OTP/verification-code lines and bail the payload if login-wall/skeleton noise present ('Email or phone','Forgot password','Create account'+'Log in'); deny-list still redacts to metadata triple for blocked apps.

GLOBAL: after reconstruction, denoise (strip object-replacement ￼, control/symbol chars, collapse blank lines) and clip to JSON_MAX_LLM_CONTEXT_CHARS keeping the HEAD (the post + earliest comments) since the comment count is small — only switch to tail-clip when 3+ speaker lines AND focus present (json_should_clip_thread_tail).

PER-APP: label this surface 'facebook-main' for the analyzer (context_prompt_smoke fixture_label maps facebook/feed→facebook-main; thresholds: min 4 lines, 250 chars, >=1 speaker-like line, is_chat_like_label=true).

steps: Open https://www.facebook.com/ in its own new Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web a11y tree (background tabs do not expose it).

Settle for SPA boot (~2.5s) then DISMISS any blocking interstitial BEFORE touching the feed — the real capture failed because a Chrome 'Remember Password' prompt and FB cookie/notification modals sat over the page. Poll up to 4s and click, in order if present: button[name='Not Now'|'Not now'], [aria-label='Close'] on any role=dialog, 'Decline optional cookies'/'Allow all cookies', 'Not Now' on the notifications prompt. (The Chrome native 'Remember Password' bubble is browser chrome, not DOM — if it blocks, it does not occlude the a11y tree, so proceed; only DOM modals need dismissal.)

Do NOT comment on the top of the wall blindly — scroll to and pick the FIRST real feed post that already has rendered comments. POLL (waitFor, 150ms cadence, up to 20s) for a post article: 'div[role=\'article\']' that is NOT [aria-label*='Sponsored'] and NOT a create-story/composer card; scrollIntoView it.

Within that post, expand the comment thread if collapsed: click 'View more comments' / 'NN comments' link if present (waitFor up to 3s, optional) so at least 1-2 existing comments are in the DOM. Then open the comment composer by clicking the post's comment affordance — POLL for the inline comment box [aria-label='Leave a comment'] (or [aria-label^='Comment as'] / [aria-label='Write a comment']) up to 12s and click it; this is the element the harness already targets.

Re-seat the caret right before the UIA read (focus is lost to FB re-renders): ensureExpr against the composeSelector — query the contenteditable comment box, click + focus + collapse a Range to its end, retry up to 4×400ms, and verify document.activeElement matches. POLL on activeElement matching the selector, do not fixed-sleep.

Page.bringToFront once more, resolve the Chrome window HWND by title 'Facebook' (resolve-hwnd.ps1, occlusion-proof; strip leading unread '(N) ' count), then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <hwnd> and --selection --hwnd <hwnd>.

Feed the --tree snapshot through context_prompt_smoke --label facebook-main --require-prompt-json and assert quality.replyContextReady (composer field + >=1 speaker line + >=250 chars + no login/otp noise).
recipe.timingNotes: Everything is POLLED, not fixed-slept, except the ~2.5s SPA settle. POLL targets and their typical delays: blocking DOM modals (cookie/notify/Save-info) appear 0.5-2s after load — poll+dismiss up to 4s before interacting. A real feed post article (div[role='article'] with comments) renders 2-6s after the wall paints — waitFor up to 20s. The inline comment box [aria-label='Leave a comment'] does NOT exist until the post is engaged — waitFor up to 12s AFTER clicking the comment affordance. 'View more comments' is optional, poll only 3s. Final focus verification polls document.activeElement matching the composeSelector (retry 4×400ms) because FB re-renders steal focus. KNOWN FAILURE (from this capture run): if no post comment box is opened the [aria-label='Leave a comment'] never appears and the snapshot comes back empty (rawSnapshot.json was 0 bytes, prompt_empty) — the recipe MUST confirm the composer is engaged before the UIA read, analogous to Gmail polling span.ams.bkH (~3-4s) before the reply body exists.

targetJson.description: Clean JSON for a Facebook feed comment composer. The LLM receives the single post being acted on (author + post text), the existing comment thread with per-author attribution, the user's reply target (the post, or a specific comment when in a nested reply box), and the user's current draft. ZERO feed/nav/ad/engagement noise. participants[] lists every distinct author so the model knows who is in the thread; conversation[] is ordered oldest→newest with the post first, each comment attributed. Maps onto the existing flat prompt JSON as: app/url/window/field metadata + a single 'screen' string holding 'Author: body' turns (post first, then comments), with the draft inferred from the focused composer; the structured shape below is the ideal the formatter should converge toward.
targetJson.exampleJson:
{
  "app": "chrome.exe",
  "url": "https://www.facebook.com/",
  "window": "Facebook",
  "field": "Leave a comment",
  "surface": "facebook-feed-comment",
  "threadSubject": "Post by Nina Reyes",
  "participants": ["Nina Reyes", "Omar Haddad", "Priya Shah", "You"],

  ],
  "replyTarget": {"kind": "post", "author": "Nina Reyes"},
  "draft": ""
}

specs.surfaceSummary: User has a WhatsApp Web chat open and dictates "reply to this" with the caret in the message composer; the LLM must receive the recent conversation (per-author turns) for the open chat plus the current draft, with the chat-list/sidebar/calls noise stripped.
specs.multiAuthor: WhatsApp Web exposes the message log as a content list (UIA `list`, web name "Messages") whose children are per-message rows. Author attribution comes in two shapes the extractor already reconstructs: (A) a grouped shape — `<group name="Sarah Chen"><text>line1</text><text>line2</text></group>` where the GROUP's `name` is the author and consecutive `<text>` children are that author's message lines (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns prefixes each with `author: `); and (B) a per-row shape — `<item>`/`<row>` whose first text value is a sender label. The user's own turns surface under the synthetic author "You". Two-party chats name most turns "You" vs the contact's display name; WhatsApp groups carry the real per-member display name on each turn (this is the Discord-server-equivalent case where attribution is load-bearing). Drop any node whose text is a date/time/status line (today/yesterday at H:MM, H:MM am/pm, "online", "typing…") and any system row (joined/left/missed call/changed group name) before attribution. The composer is NOT in this list — its UIA name is "Type a message" (role `doc`/textbox on web, `edit` in the native app) and must never be emitted as a turn.
noiseToStrip: Left sidebar chat list — the roster of OTHER conversations (UIA list name 'Chats'/'Chat list' with rows like 'Mom. Did you eat? 8:15 AM'); only the OPEN conversation's message log belongs in context
Per-message timestamps and read receipts (e.g. '8:15 AM', 'today at 2:14 PM', double-check ticks, 'Delivered'/'Read')
Presence/typing status lines under the chat header ('online', 'last seen today at …', 'typing…')
System / activity rows: 'Messages and calls are end-to-end encrypted', '<name> changed the group name', '<name> added/removed <name>', 'Missed voice call', 'Missed video call', '<name> joined/left'
Composer toolbar + affordances: emoji button, attach/clip, voice-message mic, send button, and the placeholder element itself ('Type a message')
Chat header action buttons: video call, voice call, search-in-chat, menu (⋮), and the contact/group name shown as the window title (focus-miss guard)

Browser chrome from the native --tree capture: window/pane title bar buttons (Minimize/Maximize/Close/Back/Forward/Reload), the omnibox <edit name='Address and search bar'>, and the <toolbar name='Bookmarks'>
Global search box ('Search or start a new chat'), filter tabs (All / Unread / Favourites / Groups), and the new-chat/community/status nav rail
Object-replacement / control glyphs and emoji-only decoration runs (\p{C}, bullets, pictographs) collapsed by denoise_for_llm
Login/QR-skeleton text when not authenticated ('Scan to log in', 'Download WhatsApp for Windows', 'Log in with phone number') — must be detected as not-usable, never sent as context
pruneRules: GLOBAL: focused-field-first. Find the focus path (json_find_focus_path on focus="1"); if the focused node is the composer (role doc/edit named 'Type a message'/'Message'), climb to the nearest CONTENT landmark on that path (json_find_landmark_on_path over roles doc/pane/group/article) and scope extraction to it — this lands on WhatsApp's <pane name='Conversation'> not the whole window.

GLOBAL: when no usable landmark is on the focus path, fall back to the LARGEST content landmark by scoped clean-text length (json_find_largest_landmark). For WhatsApp this still resolves to the conversation pane because the chat-list pane is dropped/sparse after nav pruning.

GLOBAL: drop whole subtrees by role before emitting (json_drop_subtree_role): toolbar, tabs/tab, menu/menuitem, status, button, link, combo, check, radio, image, tree, table, thead, banner. This removes the composer toolbar, call/search/menu buttons, the bookmarks toolbar, and the chat header action bar.
GLOBAL: drop the omnibox edit (json_is_omnibox: role edit with name 'address and search bar'/'search'/'search mail'/'urlbar') so the Chrome URL bar never leaks as field text.

GLOBAL content-vs-nav list disambiguation (json_is_nav_chrome): a list is nav (dropped) when its name matches the nav regex (chats?/conversations?/inbox/channels/members/navigation/recents/threads/sidebar/…) UNLESS it also matches the content-list regex (messages?/conversation with/message thread/comments?/timeline) OR it contains the focused node. WhatsApp's content list is named 'Messages' → kept; the sidebar list named 'Chats' → dropped. PER-APP OVERRIDE: also treat a PANE/GROUP literally named 'Chat list' (or 'Chats') that does NOT contain focus as nav chrome — extend JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE with 'chat list'|'chats' so the sidebar pane is dropped by name rather than relying solely on largest-landmark winning (today it only survives because the conversation pane is bigger).

GLOBAL multi-author reconstruction (json_reconstruct_speaker_turns): for each item/group/row in the content list, take the node name (or first text child) as the author via json_normalize_author, gather descendant text values, drop time/meta/low-signal lines, and emit 'author: message' per remaining line. Authors that match nav/container/content-list names or low-signal UI are rejected. This produces the per-turn attribution the gate counts as speakerLike.

GLOBAL noise-line filtering (json_is_time_or_meta_line + json_is_low_signal_ui_line): drop today/yesterday at H:MM (a|p)m, bare H:MM am/pm, relative ages (5m/2h/3d), online/offline/typing…, and the join/left/started-a-call/missed-call/pinned/reacted/changed-channel-name/added-to/removed-from system rows. PER-APP NOTE: WhatsApp's 'Missed voice call'/'Missed video call' and 'Messages and calls are end-to-end encrypted' are covered by the existing system-row alternation; verify the encryption-notice line is dropped (it has no speaker prefix and is low-signal but not in the explicit list — may need a per-app term).

GLOBAL: prefer the composer's caret split when the focused field is itself rich (json_focused_field_is_rich over textBefore/textAfter/focusedText ≥ 40 chars) and emit beforeCaret/afterCaret instead of the tree. For a 'reply to this' with an EMPTY draft the field is thin, so the pruned axHtml tree path (screen) is what carries the conversation — keep the tree path enabled for WhatsApp.

GLOBAL clip policy: when ≥3 speaker-prefixed lines are present and focus is known (json_should_clip_thread_tail), keep the RECENT TAIL (clip_tail to 12k chars) so the latest turns nearest the composer survive; otherwise clip_head. WhatsApp threads should keep the tail.

GLOBAL privacy/usability gate (mirrored in the smoke analyzer, not the extractor): reject as unusable when the QR/login skeleton is present (login_or_skeleton_noise) or OTP/verification-code noise appears; the WhatsApp QR screen must yield contextPayloadUsable=false rather than a bogus reply context.

steps: Open https://web.whatsapp.com/ in its own foreground Chrome window (Target.createTarget newWindow:true) and Page.bringToFront so Chrome builds the web accessibility tree (background tabs don't expose UIA content).

PRECONDITION: the dedicated capture browser MUST already be logged in (persistent profile, 'Stay logged in on this browser' checked). WhatsApp Web is QR-gated — if the page shows 'Scan to log in' / the QR canvas, the capture is invalid; poll for the chat grid and bail to a not-usable result if it never appears. (This is exactly why the current artifacts/context-cdp/whatsapp capture came back empty.)

POLL (do not fixed-sleep) for the chat list to render: waitFor("div[role='grid'] div[role='row']", up to 60000ms) — WhatsApp's first paint after session-restore is slow; the 60s ceiling in the existing recipe is deliberate.
Click the first chat row (the matched div[role='row']) to open a real conversation, then wait ~900ms for the message pane to mount.

POLL for the composer: waitFor("div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox']", up to 15000ms). On WhatsApp Web this is the 'Type a message' box (distinct from the search box, which is also contenteditable — the message composer is the one inside the conversation footer; if multiple match, prefer the one whose aria-label/placeholder is 'Type a message').
Seat the caret: focus the composer, selectNodeContents+collapse(false) to put the caret at the end (focusEl helper).

Re-assert focus right before the UIA read with ensureExpr(composeSelector) (click+focus+caret-to-end, retry ×4 at 400ms) because WhatsApp re-renders the footer and can steal focus; verify document.activeElement matches the composer.

Page.bringToFront once more, resolve the window HWND by title (resolve-hwnd.ps1, occlusion-proof; title sanitized — WhatsApp title can be '(3) WhatsApp', strip the leading unread count), then run winstt-context.exe --tree --hwnd <HWND> and --selection --hwnd <HWND>.

Feed the --tree snapshot to context_prompt_smoke --label whatsapp --require-prompt-json and assert quality.replyContextReady (composer focused + ≥4 context lines + ≥250 chars + ≥2 speaker-like lines, no login/OTP noise).
recipe.timingNotes: Everything is poll-based, not fixed sleeps. Three real waits: (1) chat-list grid div[role='grid'] div[role='row'] — up to 60s because session-restore first paint is the slowest of all surfaces; (2) composer div[contenteditable='true'][role='textbox'] — up to 15s after clicking a chat; (3) the ~900ms settle after the chat click is the only short fixed delay (message pane mount). Analogous to Gmail polling span.ams.bkH ~3-4s after the email opens. The hard blocker today is auth state, not timing: if div[role='grid'] never yields a row within 60s the page is on the QR/login screen and the result is invalid — surface that as not-usable rather than reading a QR-screen tree. The native helper's own 750ms watchdog + 1200ms outer fence (READ_TIMEOUT_MS) bound the UIA read.

Emitted today via the generic context fragment: app/url/window/field metadata + a 'screen' string carrying author-prefixed turns (one 'Author: message' per line) reconstructed from the conversation list; 'selection'/'beforeCaret'/'afterCaret' carry the draft when present. The fields below describe the IDEAL structured shape the extractor should converge toward.

# WinSTT Context-Awareness — 8-Surface Implementation Plan

## Grounding facts (verified against the code, not the specs)

- **`examples/winstt-electron/.../ax-prune.ts` does NOT exist** in this checkout (confirmed: `examples/` is absent). The 1:1 Rust port in `src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs` (functions `json_*`, `prune_ax_html_for_llm`) **is the current source of truth**. There is nothing to "mirror from ax-prune.ts" — A) is about extending the already-present Rust pruner.
- **The extractor is already substantially correct.** `prune_ax_html_for_llm` (context.rs:1181) + `json_reconstruct_speaker_turns` (:973) + `json_resolve_landmark` (:1168) already produce clean `Author: text` turns and pass 25+ unit tests covering Discord/Slack/Teams/Telegram/WhatsApp/Messenger/Instagram/GitHub/Notion/Zoom/Facebook shapes. The split-author and inline-`name="Author: text"` shapes both work.
- **The output contract is FLAT JSON keys** (`app`/`url`/`window`/`field`/`screen`/`beforeCaret`/`afterCaret`/`fieldText`/`selection`/`clipboard`/`screenOcr`/`note`/`ide`), emitted by `format_context_for_prompt_json` (:1331). The LLM prompt preamble (`with_context_prefix_json`, prompts.rs:458) explicitly lists these keys and **already teaches the model to read `Author:`/`You:` speaker prefixes** (lines 482-485). The smoke gate (`prompt_context_text`, context_prompt_smoke.rs:543) reads only those flat string keys and counts speaker-like lines with the same regex as `JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE`.

**Consequence — the single most important architectural decision:** The structured `participants[]`/`conversation[{author,text}]`/`replyTarget`/`draft` shape in the specs is the *logical* target, but the codebase has deliberately converged on encoding it as `Author: text\n` lines inside the existing `screen`/`beforeCaret` strings. **Do NOT add new top-level JSON keys** — the LLM preamble, the smoke gate, and 25 tests are all built on the flat contract. The win is making the flat contract *clean and consistently populated*, not bolting on a parallel structured shape. (B) below adapts accordingly.

The two real, code-confirmed defects are both about **which branch the formatter takes**, not about the pruner's quality.

---

## A) Shared global pruning fixes in `context.rs`

The pruner (`prune_ax_html_for_llm`) is good. The bug is that **rich-but-polluted caret text bypasses it.** `format_context_for_prompt_json` (:1344) routes to `json_build_content_sections` (flat `beforeCaret`) whenever `json_focused_field_is_rich` is true — and for Gmail/X the composer's UIA TextPattern range spans the whole document, so `text_before` is "rich" (>40 chars) but full of left-nav + inbox + counts. `strip_list_scrollback` (:700) only does a linear 85%-cut that provably leaves the 2-3 inbox rows adjacent to the open email (the Amazon.sa/GoDaddy/OTP leak).

**A1. Add a "rich field is actually nav-polluted scrollback" detector** — new fn in context.rs:

```rust
// True when caret text is "rich" only because the UIA range spans the whole
// page (Gmail reading-pane / X article), not because the user typed a long draft.
fn json_caret_is_page_scrollback(text: &str) -> bool {
    let lower = text.to_lowercase();
    // co-occurring browser/app nav markers that never appear in a real draft
    let nav_hits = ["inbox", "compose", "snoozed", "drafts", "promotions",
        "home", "explore", "notifications", "bookmarks", "what's happening",
        "trending", "who to follow", "address and search bar"]
        .iter().filter(|m| lower.contains(*m)).count();
    nav_hits >= 2
        || JSON_SPEAKER_PREFIX_RE.is_match(text) && text.lines().count() > 12
}
```

**A2. Re-route in `format_context_for_prompt_json` (:1344).** Change the rich-field branch so that when the snapshot has an `ax_html` tree AND (`json_caret_is_page_scrollback(before)` is true), it prefers the tree path (`json_build_fallback_tree_section`) over the flat `beforeCaret`. Real typed drafts (no nav markers, short) keep the existing fast `beforeCaret` path. This is the **highest-impact fix** and it lifts Gmail, X-reply, and Facebook-feed at once, because all three share the "rich whole-page caret" failure mode. Concretely:

```rust
let before = json_clean_caret(snapshot.text_before.as_deref());
let after  = json_clean_caret(snapshot.text_after.as_deref());
let has_tree = snapshot.ax_html.as_deref().map_or(false, |a| !a.trim().is_empty());
let polluted = json_caret_is_page_scrollback(&before);
if json_focused_field_is_rich(snapshot) && !(has_tree && polluted) {
    // existing fast path: real draft / clean reply body
    sections.extend(json_build_content_sections(snapshot));
    ...
}
// otherwise fall through to the tree-prune path (screen) below
```

**A3. Replace the linear `strip_list_scrollback` for the Gmail-leak case with a structural guard.** Keep the fn for the legacy flat path, but when the tree path runs it already scopes to the focus-path landmark via `json_find_landmark_on_path` (:1118), which structurally excludes the inbox `list` (already classified nav by `json_is_nav_chrome`). The one gap to close: A2 ensures Gmail actually *reaches* that code. Add a Gmail-specific landmark refinement only if needed (D below).

**A4. Extend the noise regexes (global, benefits every surface):**
- `JSON_LOW_SIGNAL_UI_LINE_RE` (:625): add standalone `views`, `view quotes`, `show translation`, `embedded video`, `relevant`, `subscribe`, `unsubscribe`, `verified sender`, and a **bare-count alternation** `^\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?[kmb]?$` (X emits `232.9K` and `Views` on separate lines).
- `JSON_TIME_OR_META_LINE_RE` (:609): add bare month-day `^(?:Jan|…|Dec)\s+\d{1,2}$` and `^\d{1,2}:\d{2}\s?[ap]m\s+·\s+.+`, plus `^(?:active|last seen).+`, `seen`, `delivered`, `sent`, and WhatsApp's `messages and calls are end-to-end encrypted`.
- `JSON_CONTAINER_NAV_RE` (:598): add `chat list|chats` so a WhatsApp/Messenger sidebar **pane** named "Chat list" is dropped by name (not only by losing the largest-landmark contest).
- `JSON_LLM_NOISE_RE` (:583): add U+034F (CGJ), U+200C-U+200F (ZWNJ/ZWJ/LRM/RLM) so Gmail's invisible-separator preview padding collapses.

**A5. Drop the `Ad`/promoted-block line (X).** In `json_is_low_signal_ui_line` add: a line that is exactly `Ad` or ends with ` Ad` (and is not speaker-prefixed) → low-signal. This is global-safe (mirrors the existing `sponsored` rule).

All of A is in **one file, `src-tauri/src/winstt/context.rs`**, behind the existing regex/fn structure — no signature changes, so the 25 existing tests stay green and you add ~6 new ones.

---

## B) Structured multi-author assembly — DO IT VIA THE FLAT CONTRACT, not new keys

The specs ask for `participants[]`/`conversation[]`/`replyTarget`/`draft`. **Recommendation: do not add these as JSON keys.** Instead:

- **`conversation[{author,text}]` → already emitted** as the `Author: text\n` lines inside `screen` (chat/social, thin composer) or `beforeCaret` (rich reply body). The LLM preamble already parses these. This is the existing, tested behavior. Keep it.
- **`draft`** → already the `fieldText`/`beforeCaret` of the focused composer. No change.
- **`replyTarget`** → for chat/social this is the last non-`You:` line of `screen`; the LLM infers it. No structured key needed; the model is explicitly told "When composing a reply, attribute prior turns to the right speaker."
- **`participants[]`/`self`/`threadSubject`** → derivable but **low value for an LLM-cleanup prompt** and not consumed by anything today. Skip unless a future need appears.

**Which surfaces emit speaker turns (the `screen` reconstruction) vs not:**

| Surface | Path | Emits speaker turns? |
|---|---|---|
| gmail (single open email) | tree → `screen` after A2 reroute | Yes — `Sender: body` (one turn typical) |
| x-reply | tree → `screen` after A2 reroute | Yes — `displayName: body` per tweet |
| **x (compose)** | thin field → `fieldText` only | **No** — draft only, `screen` should be empty/absent |
| discord, discord-server | tree → `screen` (thin composer) | Yes — multi-author |
| facebook (Messenger) | tree → `screen` | Yes |
| facebook-feed | tree → `screen` | Yes — post + comments |
| whatsapp | tree → `screen` | Yes |

**B1. The only code change B needs:** an explicit **compose-vs-thread guard** so the X-compose surface does NOT dump the home-timeline feed as `screen`.
